label: ANALYSIS
labelTooltip: Active analysis mode: {{mode}}. Click 'Close' or press Esc to return to the chart.
titleTooltip: Source range covered by this analysis run

mode.selectedTooltip: Residual error mode: every included, non-excluded frame as recorded — RA / Dec are what's left after PHD2's corrections.
mode.rawRaTooltip: Raw RA mode: same range, but with PHD2's RA correction pulses re-added to the raw error. Shows what tracking would have looked like unguided in RA, while Dec keeps its corrections.
mode.spikeTooltip: Spike Analysis: detect outlier samples (|deviation from median| > k × σ_robust) and surface the dominant periods of the spike train. Useful for finding short-lived periodic disturbances that the regular periodic-error periodogram would smear into the noise floor.
mode.burstTooltip: Burst-period workbench: programmatic search for repeating spike clusters with a tunable preprocessing pipeline (resample → detrend → energy → envelope → autocorrelation + FFT + peak-spacing). Surfaces ranked candidates with harmonic flags. Use this when the simpler Spikes tab can't pin down the period.
mode.simpleSpikeTooltip: Bare-bones spike analyzer: linearly detrend the chosen axis, threshold at 3σ (with optional +/− side filter), FFT the sparse spike series, and report the dominant period plus the mean amplitude of the detected spikes. Two numbers, no knobs.
mode.manualSpikeTooltip: Manual spike picker: same detrend pipeline as Simple, but the user clicks individual data points to mark them as spikes. Mean period (from inter-pick spacing) and mean amplitude update live as points are added or removed. Left-click to add, right-click to remove.

spike.axisTooltip: Which axis to analyze for spikes. The spike-detection threshold and the periodogram both operate on the chosen axis only.
spike.directionTooltip: Which side(s) of the median to detect spikes on. One-sided periodics (e.g. drive-train chatter that only pulls one way) often show up cleaner when the opposite side is excluded — combining both directions can wash out the period if positive and negative arrivals have different cadences.
spike.dirBothTooltip: Detect spikes on both sides of the median (|deviation| > k × σ). Default — use this unless you suspect a one-sided disturbance.
spike.dirPositiveTooltip: Only flag spikes ABOVE the median (deviation > +k × σ). Useful when the disturbance pushes the star one way only.
spike.dirNegativeTooltip: Only flag spikes BELOW the median (deviation < −k × σ). Useful when the disturbance pushes the star one way only.
spike.kTooltip: Sigma multiplier for the spike-detection threshold. A sample is flagged as a spike when |value - median| > k × σ_robust (using MAD-derived σ so the threshold isn't inflated by the spikes themselves).
spike.kSliderTooltip: Adjust the sigma multiplier (1 = loose, 6 = strict). Default 3.
spike.hiFreqFilterTooltip: High-frequency filter for the top-3 list. Slider value is the minimum period (seconds) you'll see — periods shorter than this (higher-frequency components) are excluded. Drag right to filter out more high-frequency content. 0 = no filter.
spike.hiFreqFilterSliderTooltip: Drag right to exclude more high-frequency periods from the top-3. Slider value = minimum period in seconds; anything shorter is dropped. Default 8s drops Nyquist-floor noise; ~30s also drops PHD2's algorithmic-echo at 10-20s.
spike.noPeriods: No spike periods pass the high-frequency filter. Try lowering the filter or the sigma threshold.
spike.periodTitle: Period #{{index}}: detected spike period at {{period}}s, {{aligned}} events phase-aligned
spike.runStatsTooltip: Total spike events detected at the current threshold, and the robust σ used to derive that threshold. Robust σ uses the median absolute deviation rather than the ordinary standard deviation so it isn't biased upward by the spikes themselves.

burst.axisTooltip: Which guiding axis to analyze for bursts.
burst.directionTooltip: Sign filter applied BEFORE the energy transform. ± keeps both positive and negative deviations; + or − isolates one side, useful when the disturbance pushes the star one way only.
burst.energyTooltip: Spike-energy transform that turns positive/negative deviations into a non-negative signal whose envelope can be searched for periodic clusters. |x| is the simplest; x² emphasizes large excursions; RMS is a rolling root-mean-square that catches multi-sample bursts that don't peak high in any single frame.
burst.normalizeTooltip: Divide the detrended signal by 1.4826 × MAD before computing energy, so the y-scale of every downstream stage is in robust σ units rather than raw pixels. Helps when comparing across sessions with different noise floors.
burst.highPassTooltip: High-pass filter cutoff in seconds — subtracts a running median over this window so slow baseline drift doesn't masquerade as a long-period burst. 0 = off.
burst.lowPassTooltip: Low-pass smoothing window in seconds — applies a centered boxcar to the detrended signal to suppress single-sample spikes that aren't part of a burst cluster. 0 = off.
burst.envelopeTooltip: Smoothing window applied to the spike-energy series. Shapes the envelope into burst-scale clusters rather than per-sample spikes — autocorrelation works much better on a smoothed envelope.
burst.peakProminenceTooltip: Minimum height of an envelope peak above its adjacent valleys, measured in robust σ units of the envelope. Higher = stricter peak detection.
burst.peakThresholdTooltip: Minimum height of an envelope peak above the envelope median, in robust σ units. Set to 0 to keep every prominence-passing peak; raise to suppress small bumps.
burst.minSpacingTooltip: Minimum spacing between adjacent envelope peaks, in seconds. Greedy peak picking keeps the tallest peak in each cluster and drops anything within this window.
burst.periodMinTooltip: Lower bound of the period search range, in seconds. Autocorrelation lags below this are ignored when ranking candidates.
burst.periodMaxTooltip: Upper bound of the period search range, in seconds. Set to about half the source range to reliably detect 5+ cycles.
burst.noCandidates: No candidate periods found. Try widening the period range, lowering the peak prominence/threshold, or adjusting the envelope smoothing.
burst.runStatsTooltip: Number of envelope peaks the current settings find, and the resampled cadence used by the analysis. Cadence comes from the median dt of the source data.
burst.resetTooltip: Reset every Burst-tab knob to its default value and re-run the analysis. Useful when you've tuned far from a sensible baseline and want to start over.
burst.autoBestTooltip: Highest displayed confidence the running auto-adjust search has observed since the last Auto adjust click. Resets when you start a new run; persists after Stop until the next run.
burst.autoAdjustTooltip: Start a continuous multi-axis search over the numeric knobs (HP, smooth, prominence, threshold, min spacing) to maximize the top candidate's confidence. Runs indefinitely — sliders move through coordinated 2-3 knob perturbations with simulated-annealing acceptance and periodic random restarts. The 'Best so far' chip shows progress live. Click 'Stop' to halt; if the best the search saw differs from where it ends, you'll be asked which to keep.
burst.autoStopTooltip: Stop the running search. If the best configuration found is better than where the search ends, you'll be offered the choice to restore it.

settle.label: Search stopped
settle.message: Best confidence found: {{best}}%. Search ended at {{current}}%.
settle.keepBestTooltip: Apply the slider values that produced the best confidence ({{best}}%) the search ever saw.
settle.keepCurrentTooltip: Leave the sliders where the search ended ({{current}}%); discard the global-best configuration.

simpleSpike.axisTooltip: Which guiding axis to analyze. RA detrend + 3σ on RA; Dec detrend + 3σ on Dec.
simpleSpike.directionTooltip: Which side(s) of the baseline (median after detrend) to count as spikes. ± keeps both signs; + only above the baseline; − only below.
simpleSpike.dirBothTooltip: Both sides — flag samples whose |deviation| exceeds 3σ.
simpleSpike.dirPositiveTooltip: Positive side only — flag samples whose deviation exceeds +3σ.
simpleSpike.dirNegativeTooltip: Negative side only — flag samples whose deviation falls below −3σ.
simpleSpike.periodTooltip: Dominant period of the spike train, derived from FFT of the sparse spike-only series (samples below the 3σ threshold are zeroed out).
simpleSpike.amplitudeTooltip: Mean of |deviation from baseline| across the samples that exceeded the 3σ threshold. Roughly: how big is a typical spike.
simpleSpike.runStatsTooltip: Total samples flagged as spikes (above the 3σ threshold, with the active direction filter applied), and the robust σ used to set the threshold.

manualSpike.axisTooltip: Which guiding axis to analyze. Selections are remembered per-axis: switching to Dec preserves your RA picks (and vice versa). Reset clears both at once.
manualSpike.resetTooltip: Clear all selected points across BOTH axes and start over.
manualSpike.thresholdTooltip: Auto-select every sample on the active axis whose detrended Y crosses this line (in arc-seconds). Positive picks samples at or above the line on the positive side of the median; negative picks samples at or below on the negative side. Replaces — not adds to — the active-axis pick set.
manualSpike.thresholdSliderTooltip: Drag to move the cyan preview line on the chart. Range covers the full ± y-extent of the detrended trace in arc-seconds, in 0.01″ steps. Resets to the middle (0) on axis change. Click Select to commit the current line into a pick set.
manualSpike.thresholdValueTooltip: Current threshold value in arc-seconds (two-decimal precision). Brightens once it moves off 0, indicating Select can be clicked.
manualSpike.selectTooltip: Commit the current threshold line — selects every sample on the active axis whose deviation from the median crosses the line (positive value = above; negative value = below). Replaces the current active-axis selection; the other axis is untouched. Disabled while the slider sits at 0.
manualSpike.gestureHint: left-click = add point · right-click = remove point · scroll = X zoom · drag = X pan + Y zoom
manualSpike.hoverTooltip: Detrended value under the cursor. The vertical line tracks the cursor on the chart; the value shows here. Move the mouse off the chart to clear.

allFramesTooltip: Choose whether the FFT runs on every included frame, or only those left after the auto-applied dither/settling exclusion. The original PHD2 desktop viewer never auto-excluded — flip this on to match its analysis output.
allFramesOnTooltip: Currently using every included frame. Click to re-apply the dither/settling exclusion to the FFT.
allFramesOffTooltip: Currently excluding auto-detected dither/settling frames from the FFT. Click to include them (matches the original desktop's behavior).
yLockTooltip: Pin the periodogram Y-axis maximum so residual-error and raw-RA peaks can be compared at the same scale across mode switches and ARCSEC↔PIXELS rescales. Drag-Y is disabled while locked.
yLockSetTooltip: Lock the periodogram Y-axis at the current zoom level. Subsequent mode swaps and unit changes won't rescale the axis; drag-Y is disabled until you release.
yLockClearTooltip: Release the Y-axis lock; you can drag-Y to zoom again. The previous zoom level is preserved.
resetYTooltip: Drop the manual Y-zoom and let the periodogram autorange. The reset is gated when the axis is locked — release the lock first.
topGraphTooltip: Show or hide the drift graph above the periodogram. When hidden, the periodogram expands to fill the space.
gestureHint: scroll = X zoom · drag = X pan + Y zoom · right-drag = Y pan + X zoom · hover = vertical cursor + readout
maxPeriodTooltip: Excludes any peak whose period exceeds this many seconds; 600s (10 min) is the default since PE worm cycles are typically well below that.
primaryPeriodTooltip: The reference period that all period ratios divide by (and the cap on the top-3 list). Auto-filled with the dominant peak the first time this log is analyzed; edit it to your own value. Saved per guide log — it persists across sections and reloads, and a different log recalculates.
resetToAutoTooltip: Recompute the primary period from the dominant peak of the current section.
noPeaks: No periodogram peaks under {{seconds}}s detected. Try raising the max-period filter or check the signal.
peakTitle: Peak {{index}}: a periodic-error component at {{period}}s period

debugViewer.title: Debug log
debugViewer.matchTooltip: Clicked sample time → closest debug-log line
debugViewer.goToMatchTooltip: Scroll back to the matched line
debugViewer.openFolder: Open the folder to find it automatically
debugViewer.openFolderTooltip: Grant access to the folder that holds the guide + debug logs; the matching PHD2_DebugLog is found and opened in a new tab. The folder is remembered for the rest of the session.
debugViewer.dropZone: …or drag the PHD2_DebugLog file here / click to choose it
debugViewer.dropZoneTooltip: Drop the sibling PHD2_DebugLog .txt file, or click to choose it — it opens in the new tab scrolled to the matching line.

error.notFound: Couldn't find the matching debug log. The browser can't read a folder from a dragged/picked guide log — so either drag the guide log AND its PHD2_DebugLog file in together next time, or grant the folder / pick the file below.
error.notDebugLog: That file doesn't look like a PHD2 debug log (its first line should contain 'begins execution' or 'continues execution'). Pick the correct PHD2_DebugLog file.
error.noTimestamp: This log has no parseable start time, so the sample can't be matched to a debug-log timestamp.
error.empty: The debug log appears to be empty.
error.popupBlocked: Your browser blocked the new tab. Allow pop-ups for this site, then double-click the sample again.
